objective: Load and render one real, locally bundled VRM 1.0 development avatar in the sandboxed Electron/Vue stage through pinned three.js and @pixiv/three-vrm dependencies. The renderer must keep the code-native fallback, bind deterministic expression and motion presentation to the existing renderer-safe avatar state, prove the VRM load in Electron smoke, and preserve the sample model's embedded license and upstream provenance without claiming that the sample is Hina's final identity asset.
non_goals: Do not claim the upstream development sample is the final Hina design or an original Hina asset.
Do not download an avatar at runtime, load a user-selected arbitrary path or expose a generic filesystem/network bridge.
Do not modify the conversation, speech, memory, safety or avatar backend policy.
Do not report phoneme-accurate lip sync or audio-amplitude binding in Electron; M07-S2 browser amplitude evidence remains the only real amplitude path in this iteration.
Do not add camera tracking, face tracking, Live2D, OBS integration, packaging or auto-update.
Do not run dropped-frame, A/V soak or eight-hour release gates in fast-development mode.
Do not mark M07 complete or open M08.
contracts: The only bundled VRM is the exact upstream VRM Consortium sample pinned by repository commit and SHA-256; embedded VRM meta must allow everyone avatar use, corporate commercial use, redistribution and modification redistribution.
The UI names the model as an official development sample and never calls it the final Hina avatar; production_ready remains false.
GLTFLoader registers VRMLoaderPlugin, loads only the fixed relative bundled asset and never accepts a URL/path from IPC, environment, query string, user input or backend data.
VRM render failure leaves the existing code-native SVG visible with a bounded error; success sets a renderer-local loaded signal without changing the backend asset truth.
Avatar state and expression props influence deterministic visual presentation only; renderer business policy remains in backend modules.
Electron CSP permits only same-origin bundled resources and continues to deny remote navigation, popup, webview and direct HTTP control-plane access.
Animation frame handles, ResizeObserver, renderer, scene resources and VRM resources are disposed when the component unmounts.
Electron smoke must wait for both typed preload IPC and the real VRM loader success signal.
allowed_dependencies: @pixiv/three-vrm 3.5.5 (MIT), exact renderer dependency and its npm-locked transitive @pixiv packages.
VRM1_Constraint_Twist_Sample.vrm from vrm-c/vrm-specification commit 3942748efbc803b258e288e0f6c993c6bb96cebf, embedded VRM Public License 1.0 settings.
acceptance_criteria: Dependency lock and code provenance contain exact npm versions, integrity, source repositories, licenses and no-copy statements.
Avatar manifest records upstream commit, source URL, SHA-256, size, embedded author/copyright/license settings and development-only promotion state.
Vue typecheck and build pass with Three.js and three-vrm.
Unit/static tests cover all six avatar states, unknown expression neutral fallback, fixed asset URL, cleanup and the absence of runtime remote/user-controlled asset selection.
Electron smoke loads the 10,776,032-byte bundled VRM through the real renderer and returns vrmLoaded true after typed IPC succeeds.
When the fixed model fails, the existing SVG remains usable and the desktop displays an honest bounded error.
Governance/provenance tests validate the binary hash and embedded VRM license metadata.
README explains that the bundled avatar is a replaceable official development sample, not Hina's final character design.
required_test_commands: uv run --frozen python -m unittest discover -s tests/governance -p "test_*.py"
